prose.title: 91m mos
prose.body:
The 91M Military Working Dog (MWD) Handler is a military occupational specialty (MOS) in the United States Army. MWD Handlers are responsible for the care, training, and deployment of military working dogs. They work closely with their canine partners to detect explosives, search for narcotics, conduct patrols, and provide security.

To become a 91M MOS, individuals must complete the necessary training requirements. This includes Basic Combat Training (BCT) and Advanced Individual Training (AIT) at the Military Working Dog Handler School, located at Lackland Air Force Base in San Antonio, Texas. During AIT, soldiers will learn about canine care, obedience training, and how to work with their working dog.

Upon completing the training, MWD Handlers can be assigned to various units in the Army, including infantry units, special operations units, and military police units. They may be deployed overseas or stationed at domestic military installations.

In addition to working with their canine partners, MWD Handlers also provide veterinary care, maintain equipment, and conduct training exercises to keep their dogs sharp and ready for missions.

Overall, the 91M MOS is a rewarding and challenging career path for those passionate about working with animals and serving in the military.
